top3.title: Philadelphia Psychotronic Film Society
top3.why: PhilaMOCA's Psychotronic Film Society is a monthly plunge into forgotten, deranged, gloriously bad cinema — exactly the kind of weird-culture screening that never surfaces on streaming. It's a signature night at Philly's best home for underground film, and at $5 at the door it's the cheapest good time of the week. If you only do one thing Monday, do this.

top3.title: Mississippi Law: Policing and Reform in America's Jim Crow Countryside
top3.why: Justin Randolph traces the line from slave patrols to state highway patrols to mass incarceration — a genealogy of Jim Crow rule and racial authoritarianism in the rural South. It's the week's sharpest political reading, hosted free at the anarchist Wooden Shoe. The kind of talk that reframes how you read every headline afterward.

top3.title: Kirk Whalum
top3.why: Kirk Whalum is a Grammy-winning saxophonist whose gospel-and-soul roots run straight through the Philadelphia sound. Seeing him in City Winery's intimate room is a world away from the arena shows across town this same night. A rare chance to catch a genuine soul-jazz craftsman up close.

events.title: Five Finger Death Punch
events.note: Metal tour stop at the waterfront amphitheater.

events.title: Kirk Whalum
events.note: Grammy-winning gospel-jazz saxophonist — a rare archival-soul-adjacent set in an intimate room.

events.title: J. Cole: The Fall-Off Tour
events.note: Hip-hop arena show. (Also listed on Songkick.)

events.title: Sound of America (musical)
events.note: Ongoing musical running July 1–August 1; spans the full target week.

events.title: Philadelphia Psychotronic Film Society
events.note: Monthly deep-dive into forgotten, deranged, gloriously bad cinema you won't find on streaming. $5 at the door.

events.title: Mississippi Law: Policing and Reform in America's Jim Crow Countryside
events.note: Justin Randolph on rural law enforcement as the infrastructure of US racial authoritarianism from slave patrols to state highway patrols. Free.

events.title: Coffee Talk
events.note: First & third Monday social hangout for trans and non-binary people, partners, and allies. Snacks and coffee. (Recurring.)

events.title: Dark Side Burlesque's Devil Fruits: A Burlesque & Drag Tribute to One Piece
events.note: Burlesque and drag tribute to the anime One Piece.

top3.title: BLEEDER (BMG), LOVERGIRL (MPLS), MYSTERY DUNGEONS, B00B
top3.why: A four-band DIY bill running from emo-chiptune to emotional hardcore, tucked into a West Philly house venue where the barrier between stage and floor doesn't exist. Two of the acts are touring through, so this exact lineup won't come around again. NOTAFLOF and mask-required — pure Philly basement-punk ethos, pay what you can.

top3.title: Aloïse
top3.venue: Lightbox Film Center (Village of Industry & Art)
top3.why: Lightbox unveils a fresh restoration of 'Aloïse' — the kind of resurrected art film you can only really see on a proper screen with a room full of people who care. Lightbox is the city's most reliable repertory house, and restoration nights here are always the cinephile move. A quiet, singular alternative to the week's louder options.

top3.title: Stabbing Westward, Priest, & Acumen Nation
top3.why: Stabbing Westward built the '90s industrial-rock blueprint, and they're rolling through with Priest and Acumen Nation for a full night of dark synth aggression. Industrial packages this stacked rarely stop in Philly. Confirm the venue and time before you go, but if the goth-industrial itch is real, this scratches it.

events.title: BLEEDER (BMG), LOVERGIRL (MPLS), MYSTERY DUNGEONS, B00B
events.note: Emo-chiptune / emotional hardcore / punk bill with out-of-town bands. NOTAFLOF, mask required, all-ages DIY.

events.title: Stabbing Westward, Priest, & Acumen Nation
events.note: Industrial/goth touring bill — Stabbing Westward with Priest and Acumen Nation. (confirm details — venue/time not listed.)

events.title: Aloïse
events.venue: Lightbox Film Center (Village of Industry & Art)
events.note: New restoration of 'Aloïse' at Philly's premier repertory/arthouse cinema.

events.title: The Writing Room
events.note: Co-work with other writers in three 40-minute silent-writing units with check-ins. Any project welcome. Free.

events.title: In-person! Vogue Practice Session
events.note: Free drop-in vogue practice, 6–9pm. (Recurring weekly community event.)

events.title: SHARK SIDE OF THE MOON (stream)
events.note: Group stream of the 2022 shark-in-space B-movie — a birthday pick, watched together online.

top3.title: PAVEMENTS (2024)
top3.why: Alex Ross Perry's 'Pavements' is a gleeful meta-prank of a music film — part biopic, part hoax, part museum piece — and it's screening at PhilaMOCA the same weekend Pavement actually plays the Dell. R5 and WXPN tie it to the Make The World Better weekend, so the whole city's slack-rock energy converges here. Catch the myth before you catch the band.

top3.title: Bride (NOLA), Kate Ferencz, Annie Achron
top3.why: New Orleans' Bride brings minimal-synth and experimental textures north for a bill with Kate Ferencz and Annie Achron, hosted at the artist-run Space 1026. It's the kind of touring underground night that lives entirely by word of mouth. An easy one to miss and exactly the sort worth catching.

top3.title: DJ Basixxx with Mixxx
top3.why: Iffy Books teaches you to DJ on Mixxx — free, open-source software you run off a laptop keyboard, no gear required — for a suggested $5–15. It's the overlap of DIY, open-source tooling, and cheap hands-on learning that makes Iffy the best small room in the city. Walk in curious, walk out beatmatching.

honorable_mentions.title: Moth Night at the Schuylkill Center

events.title: Moth Night at the Schuylkill Center for Environmental Education
events.note: Nocturnal nature: a bright light rigged to draw moths and other night insects. Bring a chair, headlamp, bug spray, and curiosity.

events.title: Parks on Tap at Historic Rittenhouse Town
events.note: Roving pop-up beer garden at Historic Rittenhouse Town, running July 22–26. Free to enter.

events.title: UNCLE BOONMEE WHO CAN RECALL HIS PAST LIVES (2010) (stream)
events.note: Group stream of Apichatpong Weerasethakul's Palme d'Or winner — ghosts, forest spirits, and a dying man's last days.

top3.title: Etheric Realms of the Night
top3.why: A film premiere with a live Q&A at PhilaMOCA — 'Etheric Realms of the Night' leans into exactly the occult, dark-underground register the venue does best. Premieres like this exist for one night with the makers in the room, then vanish. It's the week's most atmospheric film event, and PhilaMOCA is the right church for it.

top3.title: RASKOL RECORD RELEASE // TOUR KICKOFF w/ GASKET, SPIKEPIT, BLUNT FORCE
top3.why: Raskol drops a new record and kicks off a tour the same night, with Gasket, Spikepit, and Blunt Force filling out a hardcore-noise bill at Cousin Danny's. Record-release shows are the peak of the DIY calendar — the band's most invested crowd, the freshest material. This is West Philly punk at full volume.

top3.title: Beginner Soldering: Li-Ion Battery Pack
top3.why: Iffy Books walks total beginners through soldering a rechargeable Li-ion pack for a LoRa node or microcontroller — real hands-on hardware for a $3.99 kit and a pay-what-you-can donation. It's practical maker skill-building you actually leave with, not just watch. The cheapest useful thing you'll do all week.

events.title: GOOD POLLUTION CASSETTE RELEASE SHOW
events.note: Art-punk / noise-rock / post-punk tape release at a South Philly DIY spot.

events.title: Slippers
events.note: With Chancers and The Woulds, in the delightfully odd Philly Typewriter shop. All ages.

events.title: RASKOL RECORD RELEASE // TOUR KICKOFF w/ GASKET, SPIKEPIT, BLUNT FORCE
events.note: Hardcore/noise/punk record release and tour kickoff with Gasket, Spikepit, and Blunt Force.

events.note: Indie show at MilkBoy. (confirm details — time not listed.)

events.venue: Urban Village Brewing Company, 1001 N 2nd St

events.title: fundraiser for get a gato! <3
events.note: Fundraiser for the Get A Gato cat cafe/rescue — half cats, half coloring and crafts. Bring socks.

events.title: Beginner Soldering: Li-Ion Battery Pack
events.note: Build a rechargeable pack for a LoRa node or microcontroller. Perfect for total soldering beginners; kit $3.99, donation optional.

events.title: EMP Range Day
events.note: Homelab and cyber-range showcase: play in an emulated IoT range with the EMP Crew's portable kit and robots.

top3.title: JAWBREAKER: A Documentary About Anti-Cimex
top3.why: A full documentary on Anti-Cimex, the Swedish band that basically invented Scandinavian crust, screening at PhilaMOCA for $12. Film, hardcore history, and underground culture collapse into one perfect night — the rare event that hits three of your interests at once. General Speech is the right label to shepherd it; PhilaMOCA is the only room it belongs in.

top3.title: Palestine 36
top3.why: The Philly Palestine Coalition and cinéSPEAK screen 'Palestine 36,' pairing arthouse cinema with active solidarity organizing at West Philly's Studio 34. It's film as political gathering, not just viewing — the audience is the point as much as the movie. A screening with real stakes and a real community behind it.

top3.title: Pavement
top3.why: Pavement headline the 10th-anniversary Make The World Better weekend — a benefit rehabbing rec centers and parks for local kids — with Philly's own They Are Gutting A Body Of Water and Ratboys opening. It's a genuinely rare visit from an indie-rock landmark, wrapped in a cause worth the ticket. Loud, communal, and the show people will be talking about all week.

events.title: Dinner & A Movie: HER PRIVATE HELL
events.venue: Regal UA King of Prussia, 300 Goddard Blvd, King of Prussia
events.note: Horror Meetup group watch of the new sci-fi/horror 'Her Private Hell,' with dinner after.

events.title: Her Private Hell (2026)
events.note: Theatrical release: a troubled woman searches for her father as a deadly mist engulfs a futuristic metropolis. Revenge, leather, and dread.

top3.title: 'Films That Explode Like Grenades: Robert Kramer and the Search for a Radical Cinema'
top3.why: Whitney Strub presents his book on Robert Kramer — the New Left's emblematic filmmaker, co-founder of the radical Newsreel collective, director of 'Ice' and 'Milestones.' Film history, leftist politics, and independent literature meet in one free talk at the anarchist Wooden Shoe. For a cinephile with radical sympathies, nothing else this week overlaps so cleanly.

top3.title: Screenings at Sanctuary: Jaws and Sleepaway Camp
top3.why: 'Jaws' and 'Sleepaway Camp' as a late-night summer double feature in a West Philly DIY space — the platonic ideal of a July horror night. It's the kind of BYO-blanket basement screening that beats any multiplex for atmosphere. Confirm the cost and door details, then show up early for a seat.

top3.title: Kurt Vile & The Violators
top3.why: Kurt Vile — Philly's own guitar-drift hero — closes the Make The World Better weekend with local heroes They Are Gutting A Body Of Water opening, all for a benefit rehabbing neighborhood rec centers. Seeing Vile in his home city at a cause show hits differently than a normal tour stop. Warm, hometown, and worth the ticket.

events.title: Screenings at Sanctuary: Jaws and Sleepaway Camp
events.note: Summer horror double feature — 'Jaws' and 'Sleepaway Camp' — at a West Philly DIY space. (confirm details — cost not listed.)

top3.title: In-person! Philly to Palestine Fashion Show
top3.why: A fashion show, raffle, and dance party where every dollar splits between a West Philly housing-stabilization fund and a family in Gaza — mutual aid you can dance to. The Rotunda hosting makes it free-spirited and all-ages in spirit, and the cause gives it real weight. The most meaningful way to spend a Sunday night this week.

top3.title: Anarchy Summer Reading Group: 'The Utopia of Rules' by David Graeber (1st half)
top3.why: Iffy's anarchist reading group takes on the first half of David Graeber's 'The Utopia of Rules,' his sharp, funny takedown of bureaucracy. Leftist theory, independent-bookstore community, and a free seat at the table — this is Iffy at its most Iffy. Read ahead or just show up and listen.

top3.title: STOMACH BOOK
top3.why: Devi McCallion — the cult voice behind Black Dresses and Girls Rituals — brings her raw, glitchy noise-pop to PhilaMOCA alongside Bubblegum Octopus. Her shows are intense, personal, and famously hard to catch. For the experimental/noise corner of your listening, this is the night's essential.
